THE CITY OF
April I3, 1992
Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COII{II{ISSION
428 rrJtr Street, Suite 800
Sacrarnento, CA 95814
RE: REQUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE PURSUANT T0 CAL. GOV. C0DE
$83II4(b) CONCERNING CONFLICT OF INTEREST DETERII,IINATION
Dear Si r/Madam:
I. 
INTRODUCTION
The City Attorneyrs 0ffice of the City of Pomona has been authorized by
Kenneth l^lest, a duly elected member of the Potmna City Council, to request of
the FPPC formal written advice pursuant to Cal iforni a Government Code
$B3ll4(b) and the California Code of Regulations $18329(b). Councilnember
l.lest's signature proceeding this letter signifies that the City Attorney of
the City of Pomona shalI act as Mr. Uestrs authorized representative rith
respect to this request for advice. See 2 Cal . Code of Reg. $18329(b)(2)(A).
II. 
ISSUE
Can Councilrnember Kenneth llest participate and/or vote with respect to an
ord'i nance and/or contract whi ch woul d authori ze the Consol i dated Fi re
Protection District of Los Angeles County to take over and thereafter provide
fire protection services for the City of Pomona where Mr. l,lest 'is Head of
Disbursement and Payroll for the Los Angeles County Sheriffrs Department?
III. 
FACTUAL BACKGROUND
A. Proposed County Annexation of Fire Services.
The annexation of a city to a fire protection district is authorized
by the Fire Protection District Act of 1987, Section 13810 of the
California Health and Safety Code. The Conso'l idated Fire Protection
District of Los Angeles County, as administered and governed by the
L. A. County Board of Supervisors, at the behest of the City of
Pomona is studying the feasibility and financial impact of providing
fire protection services to Porona.
,

City Attomey
Citv Ha[ 505 So. Catev Aw., Bor 660, Pomona. CA 9U5c

Glasman, Colvin & Adamt 1601w. Beverly Blvd', Montebelo, CA 90610
1213) 72TBm 
FAX 1213) 727{a78


Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COI,|HISSION
Apri I '13, 1992
Page 2
The District is currently ln the process of preparing a feasibility report.
District staff in cooperation with City staff are performing the folloldng
pre-annexation analysis :
a. Evaluatlng the present fire protection and paramedic rescue services
of the City, their related costs to the city and the first insurance
ratlngs i/hich are applied to the City.
b. oescribing and comparing with the present City services those
services which the District vould provide within the Clty in the
event of an annexati on.
c. I'leasuring the cost differences to the City and changes 'in the first
insurance ratings that may result from the annexation.
d. Describing the financial and operational inpacts of the annexation
on the Dl strlct.
Preparing recormendations relating to the disposition of all
equipment and facilities uti'lized by the City in its fire protection
acti vi ti es.
oescribing the proposed operations of the District wlthin the City,
including types of responses to specific alarms, intended staffing
and utilization of equipment within the City, fire prevention
actlvities, dispatching systems, support services and the
i nvesti gati ons of fire causes.
Reviewing the qualification and procedures for transfer of City
personnel to Di stri ct employment.
e.
h. Preparing written report to the City on the foregoing investigations
and services.
s.
Based upon the report presented to the City Council contajning the above
analysis, the City Council may enter into a contract with the D.istrict for
fire protection services. An accompanying ordlnance revising the City Code
appropriately would also be necessary. The Los Angeles County Boird of
Supervisors would have to vote to ratify the contract for fire aervices on
beha'lf of the District-
B. Councilnember as County Administrator for Sheriff's Department.
Counc'ilmember Kenneth l.lest .is enployed by the County of Los Angeles as
Head- of Disbursenents and payroll for -the Sherifi,s Department. A
complete job description and c'lassiflcation standards is attached for
consl derati on.


i't
Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COI'II,IISSION
April I3, 1992
Page 3
The factual setting described above as supplennnted by the attached job
descript'lon raise the questions presented Section IV, below.
IV. 
QUESTIONS
Does Councilmember I'lest, a Los Angeles County Sheriffrs Department
Administrator, have a economic interest ln the outcome of an
ordinance and/or contract which transfers Pomonars fire services to
the Los Angeles Countyrs fire district?
If Mr. l.lest does have an economic interest is said interest direct
or indirect in nature?
Can Councilmember l{est participate in the Pomona City Councilrs
discussion and analysis of a proposed ordinance and/or contract for
fire services with the Los Angeles County fire district?
D. Can Councilmember West vote on an ordinance and/or contract for fire
services with the Los Angeles fire district?
E. Does the distinction between an ordinance (divesting the City from
f'lre serv'lce responsibility in favor of the County) and a contract
(between the City and the County setting forth conditions and
financial obligatlons concerning the transfer of service) affect the
analysis in ansnering questions IA" through rtDrrr above?
v.
CONCLUS ION
A.
o
C.
Please consider the questions presented at Section IV,
additional question(s) which may be appropriate given the
Please feel free to contact this office at (714) 620-2071 ,
Kenneth West at (714) 620-?051 , if you have any queries
matter. Thank you in advance for your time and cooperation
above, and any
facts presented.
or Counci I member
concerning thi s
in resolving the
important issues raised by this letter.
City Attor
